# Revision Rhythm

Revision is a rhythm, not a rescue mission.

The best rhythm is visible in small loops: draft, render, inspect, adjust. If any step becomes mysterious, the writer starts distrusting the tool.

A reader view can support that rhythm by showing stable revision identity without making the interface feel like an admin panel.

The current mock revision label is intentionally visible. It helps during debugging, but it may eventually need to become quieter, move to a utility area, or appear only when inspecting history.

Good UX keeps operational truth available without letting it dominate the reading experience.

The rhythm also depends on trust in the boring parts. The chapter order should not shift unless someone asked for it. A title should not disappear because a paragraph was added. A long chapter should not make the next chapter unreachable. The reader should not treat every refresh as a new book.

In this mock, the rhythm is visible as a sequence of chapters with enough substance to scroll through. Some are brief. Some are long. One contains an image. Together they make revision feel less like a set of isolated unit cases and more like a small manuscript that can be revisited.
